what_it_does: This file stores YAML frontmatter metadata for a channel-memory attachment that links a design-source channel entry to an image asset.
observations: YAML frontmatter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added timestamp
target is an image:// URI pointing to design-source/company-info/p-06
No body content beyond the frontmatter
standouts: References an image asset via an image:// URI
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata
Follows an id naming pattern att-<hash>
Uses a URI-style target field for asset referencing
concerns: No caption, alt text, or description of the image
No author or source indicated for the attachment
Target asset cannot be verified from this file alone
attachment_type 'images' is plural while only one target is listed
notes: A timestamped design-source channel-memory attachment record that points to a single image asset.
questions: Who added this attachment and why?
Does the referenced image://design-source/company-info/p-06 asset actually exist?
Why is attachment_type plural 'images' when only one target is listed?
purpose_vs_actual: No explicit purpose is stated; the file acts as a channel-memory attachment record for an image asset.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to record that a specific image asset was attached to the design-source channel memory at a known time.
novelty: Uses an image:// URI scheme as the target of a channel-memory attachment record.
contribution: Provides a dated, typed link between a channel memory entry and an image asset, preserving reference provenance for downstream fusion.
summary_for_embedding: Attachment metadata record att-f887e05f in the design-source channel links to image://design-source/company-info/p-06 and records an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To register when an image asset was attached to the design-source channel memory.
